images.caption: Figure 1:Overview of Pico.(a) Merging interference in LoRA is asymmetric: task-specificAAmatrices remain relatively separated, whileBBmatrices align along shared dominant directions across tasks. (b) Pico identifies and downscales these shared directions inBBbefore merging, then rescales the merged update to preserve its magnitude.

Figure 2:Quantitative motivation for Pico.Top row: mean pairwise overlap inAAandBBacross four domain-specific LoRA adapters, shown for the query and value projection matrices. Bottom row: average effective rank ofAAandBBacross LoRA ranks. The exact values are reported in Appendix A.

Figure 3:Representative dominance pattern in the sharedBBspectrum(query projection, layer 16, rank 16). Left: contribution of each task to the leading shared components. Right: cumulative energy in the same shared basis. The first five components account for 68.8% of the total energy.

Figure 4:Spectrum calibration and progressive merge robustness.Left: representative mergedBBspectrum for the query projection at layer 16 with Task Arithmetic at rank 16. Pico reduces the dominance of the leading shared components. Right: coding average as the merge pool grows from two to six adapters. The band summarizes the range of non-Pico baselines; Pico remains stronger throughout the progressive merge.AppendixDgives the full spectral metric definitions and exact statistics; AppendixGgives the full progressive-merge comparison.
Figure 5:Pairwise overlap at rank 64.The asymmetry betweenBBandAAholds for every domain pair, showing that the effect is not driven by averaging across tasks.

header: Appendix DDefinitions and Full Spectral Statistics of the MergedBBSpace
Appendix GProgressive LoRA Merging on Coding

images.caption: Figure 6:Progressive LoRA merging on coding benchmarks.The x-axis shows the number of merged adapters. Starting from the coding adapter, we progressively add finance, medical, math, and law adapters and report the coding average after each merge. Pico remains the strongest method at every stage.
